$comment: Neutral shipped <errno.h> (POSIX) — c14a (linux) + c15b (macOS, via the c15a per-target const/macro/typedef variant mechanism). THREE surfaces, now PER-FORMAT: (1) the `errno` lvalue object-MACRO selects its accessor by format — `(*__errno_location())` on elf/glibc, `(*__error())` on macho/Darwin; (2) each accessor carries a per-symbol `availableObjectFormats` (__errno_location=elf-only, __error=macho-only) so ONLY the active format's accessor is injected + imported — DSS imports EVERY declared shipped extern, so declaring the wrong-format accessor would plant an undefined import (glibc has no __error; libSystem has no __errno_location); the injected one resolves from the active format's `library` (libc.so.6 elf / libSystem macho). (D-SHIPPED-SYMBOL-PER-TARGET-AVAILABILITY); (3) the E* numbers: the low block (EPERM..ERANGE) AGREES between Linux and Darwin so it stays flat, but the divergent ones carry per-format `variants` (Linux asm-generic vs Darwin sys/errno.h — e.g. EAGAIN 11 vs 35, ETIMEDOUT 110 vs 60, ENOTSUP 95 vs 45, EOPNOTSUPP 95 vs 102, EOVERFLOW 75 vs 84, ENOLCK 37 vs 77). Gated availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho] (the Linux WSL run target + the macos-latest CI leg, which runs Mach-O natively). The VALUES are interop-critical — verified against the Linux errno-base.h/errno.h + the Darwin xnu sys/errno.h.
